source_intake_record.raw_question_text: 1093 A block of mass $m$ slides without friction on an inclined plane of mass $M$ which in turn is free to slide without friction on a horizontal table (Fig. 1.65). Write sufficient equations to find the motion of the block and the inclined plane. You do not need to solve these equations. (Wisconsin)

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: 1093 A block of mass $m$ slides without friction on an inclined plane of mass $M$ which in turn is free to slide without friction on a horizontal table (Fig. 1.65). Write sufficient equations to find the motion of the block and the inclined plane. You do not need to solve these equations. (Wisconsin)
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL object string representation rather than a file path or URL
caption content appears inconsistent with the question and was not used for extraction
metadata.reasoning_chain: As shown in Fig. 1.65, let $x, y$ be a coordinate frame attached to the inclined plane, whose horizontal coordinate in the laboratory frame is denoted by $X$. The forces on the block and the inclined plane are as shown in the diagram. We have for the inclined plane $M \ddot{X}=N \sin \alpha$, for the motion of the block along the $x$ direction $m(\ddot{x}+\ddot{X}\cos \alpha)=-m g \sin \alpha$, and for the motion of the block along the $y$ direction $-m \ddot{X}\sin \alpha=N-m g\cos \alpha$. These three equations for the three unknowns $N, x$ and $X$ can be solved to find the motion of the system.
